749	\begin{definition}
750	
751	\leanok
752	[Chart quotient \(u^I\)]
753	  \label{def:gr_chart_quotient}
754	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap}
755	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd}
756	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
757	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
758	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L898-L910).
759	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
760	  % "We next define a rank $d$ locally free sheaf $\U$ on $\grass(r,d)$
761	  %  together with a surjective homomorphism $\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$.
762	  %  On each $U^I$ we define a surjective homomorphism
763	  %  $u^I :\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I} \to  \oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
764	  %  by the matrix $X^I$."
765	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
766	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). On the affine chart
767	  \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) define the
768	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}\)-linear homomorphism of trivial bundles
769	  \[
770	    u^I \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}
771	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}
772	  \]
773	  given by left multiplication by the universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\)
774	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}): on global sections it is the \(R^I\)-linear
775	  map \((R^I)^{r} \to (R^I)^{d}\), \(v \mapsto X^I v\). Since the \(I\)-th minor
776	  \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\) is the identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\), the composite of
777	  \(u^I\) with the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates
778	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) is the
779	  identity; hence \(u^I\) is a split surjection of locally free sheaves, with
780	  target the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\).
781	
782	  \emph{Realisation.} The free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) and
783	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) are the finite biproducts (coproducts) of copies of
784	  the unit module \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_{U^I}\), indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\)
785	  and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Each entry \(X^I_{p,q} \in R^I\) of the universal matrix
786	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) is a global function on \(U^I = \Spec R^I\),
787	  obtained from \(R^I\) through the canonical identification
788	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R^I, \mathcal{O}) \cong R^I\); the corresponding scalar
789	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,q}) : \mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\)
790	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the \((p,q)\)-component of \(u^I\). Assembling these
791	  components over the index biproducts -- the universal property of the biproduct
792	  packaging a matrix of component morphisms into a single morphism of the free
793	  sheaves -- yields \(u^I\). This entry-by-entry assembly via the finite biproduct
794	  is the substitute for a (non-existent) Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) morphism of
795	  free sheaves'' primitive.
796	\end{definition}
797	
798	\begin{lemma}\leanok
799	[The $I$-columns of the chart quotient are the identity]
800	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
801	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_ιFree}
802	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
803	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
804	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
805	  For each \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), the chart quotient \(u^I\)
806	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) carries the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th basis section of the
807	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) -- where \(\iota_I(k)\) is the \(k\)-th
808	  element of \(I\) in increasing order -- to the \(k\)-th basis section of
809	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\); that is, the \(I\)-indexed columns of \(u^I\) form the
810	  identity:
811	  \[
812	    e_{\iota_I(k)} \,\circ\, u^I \;=\; e_k.
813	  \]
814	\end{lemma}
815	
816	\begin{proof}
817	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
818	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
819	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
820	  By the biproduct presentation of \(u^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
821	  composite \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I\) reads off the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th column of the
822	  component matrix, whose \(p\)-th entry is the scalar endomorphism
823	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)})\). The \(I\)-indexed columns of the
824	  universal matrix \(X^I\), read through the order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong
825	  I\), form the \(d \times d\) identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}),
826	  so \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\). Hence the diagonal entry is
827	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
828	  off-diagonal entries are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
829	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the resulting column is the \(k\)-th coordinate
830	  inclusion, giving \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I = e_k\).
831	\end{proof}
832	
833	\begin{lemma}\leanok
834	[The chart quotient is an epimorphism]
835	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}
836	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_epi}
837	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient}
838	  The chart quotient \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
839	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules.
840	\end{lemma}
841	
842	\begin{proof}
843	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
844	  We exhibit a section, so that \(u^I\) is a \emph{split} epimorphism and hence an
845	  epimorphism. Let \(\iota_I : \{1,\dots,d\} \hookrightarrow \{1,\dots,r\}\) be the
846	  inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, \(k \mapsto \iota_I(k)\), the
847	  \(k\)-th element of \(I\) in increasing order. It induces a morphism of free
848	  sheaves \(s_I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\), the
849	  coordinate inclusion sending the \(k\)-th basis section to the
850	  \(\iota_I(k)\)-th. We claim
851	  \[
852	    u^I \circ s_I \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}}.
853	  \]
854	  Both sides are morphisms out of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf, which is the coproduct
855	  of \(d\) copies of the unit module; by the universal property of that coproduct
856	  it suffices to check the equality on each coordinate, i.e.\ to compare component
857	  matrices. The \((p,k)\)-component of \(u^I \circ s_I\) is the entry
858	  \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)}\) of the universal matrix at the \(I\)-indexed column, which
859	  is precisely the \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\). Since that minor is the
860	  \(d \times d\) identity (the columns of \(X^I\) indexed by \(I\), read through the
861	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong I\), form the identity block --
862	  \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), one has \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\),
863	  so \(u^I \circ s_I\) has the identity component matrix and equals
864	  \(\mathrm{id}\). Hence \(s_I\) splits \(u^I\) and \(u^I\) is a split
865	  epimorphism, in particular an epimorphism.
866	\end{proof}
867	
868	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
869	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
870	
871	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
872	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
873	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
874	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
875	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
876	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
877	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
878	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
879	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
880	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
881	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
882	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.
883	
884	\begin{lemma}
885	[Self-minor Cramer inverse is the identity]
886	  \label{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_self}
887	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinorInv_self}
888	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
889	  The Cramer inverse of the self-minor is the identity matrix:
890	  \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d \times d}\).
891	\end{lemma}
892	\begin{proof}
893	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
894	  The self-minor \(X^I_I\) is the identity block
895	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), and \(1^{-1} = 1\).
896	\end{proof}
897	
898	\begin{definition}\leanok
899	[The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\)]
900	  \label{def:gr_bundleTransition}
901	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition}
902	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
903	    lem:gr_minorDet_unit, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition}
904	  Let \(I, J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) be size-\(d\) subsets and let
905	  \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\) and \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be
906	  the overlap immersion and transition of the chart cover (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The
907	  \emph{bundle transition} \(g_{I,J}\) is the isomorphism of sheaves of modules on the
908	  overlap \(U^I_J\)
909	  \[
910	    g_{I,J} \;:\;
911	      f_{IJ}^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\bigr)
912	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
913	      (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)
914	  \]
915	  induced by the invertible \(d \times d\) matrix
916	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J)\) (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
917	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor, which is invertible because
918	  \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}).
919	
920	  \emph{Realisation.} The transition is built exactly as the chart quotient
921	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}: each entry \((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q} \in R^I_J\) is a global
922	  function on the overlap, its scalar endomorphism
923	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q}\bigr)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the
924	  \((p,q)\)-component, and the universal property of the rank-\(d\) biproduct assembles
925	  these into an automorphism of the free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); the
926	  source and target pullbacks of free sheaves are identified with this
927	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\) through the free-pullback comparisons
928	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(f_{IJ}\) and \(t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI}\), conjugating
929	  the matrix automorphism into the displayed isomorphism. Invertibility of the matrix makes
930	  \(g_{I,J}\) an isomorphism.
931	\end{definition}
932	
933	\begin{lemma}\leanok
934	[Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)]
935	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}
936	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_self}
937	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
938	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
939	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
940	  The self-transition is the identity: \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\) on
941	  \(f_{II}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\). This is the module-level form of the descent
942	  condition (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\).
943	\end{lemma}
944	\begin{proof}
945	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
946	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
947	  The \(I\)-th minor of the universal matrix is the identity block, \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
948	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), so its Cramer inverse is again the
949	  identity, \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}).
950	  Hence in the biproduct presentation of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} the diagonal
951	  components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
952	  off-diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}), so
953	  the assembled automorphism is the identity of \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_I}^{\,d}\); conjugation by
954	  the free-pullback comparisons preserves the identity, giving \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\).
955	\end{proof}

957	\begin{definition}
958	[Bundle transition data over the glue datum]
959	  \label{def:gr_bundleTransitionData}
960	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransitionData}
961	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, def:scheme_modules_glue}
962	  The family of bundle transitions \((g_{I,J})\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}), repackaged
963	  over the index set of the Grassmannian glue datum \(\mathrm{theGlueData}(d,r)\) in the exact
964	  shape \(\bigl(\forall I\,J,\; f_{IJ}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \cong (t_{IJ} \circ
965	  f_{JI})^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)\) that the gluing primitive
966	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} consumes.
967	\end{definition}
968	
969	\begin{lemma}[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]
970	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}
971	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix}
972	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
973	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
974	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
975	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
976	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
977	  multiplicative cocycle identity
978	  \[
979	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
980	  \]
981	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
982	\end{lemma}
983	\begin{proof}
984	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
985	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
986	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
987	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
988	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
989	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
990	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
991	  \[
992	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
993	  \]
994	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
995	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
996	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
997	  \[
998	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
999	  \]
1000	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1001	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1002	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1003	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1004	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1005	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1006	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1007	\end{proof}
1008	
1009	\begin{lemma}[Transport and endpoint alignment of the bundle transitions]
1010	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1011	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_transport}
1012	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1013	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
1014	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1015	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) with triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\),
1016	  and let \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}, \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}, \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\) be
1017	  the base-change transports (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the
1018	  three bundle transitions to \(V_{IJK}\). Under the free-pullback comparisons
1019	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) and the endpoint bridges
1020	  (\cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}), each transported transition is identified with the
1021	  matrix automorphism of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse on the common
1022	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\); granting the matrix identity
1023	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}, the composite satisfies
1024	  \[
1025	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
1026	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}
1027	  \]
1028	  as isomorphisms of sheaves of modules on \(V_{IJK}\). This is the substantive
1029	  transport step: the alignment of the three transports' domains and codomains.
1030	\end{lemma}
1031	\begin{proof}
1032	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1033	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
1034	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1035	  Each bundle transition is, by \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, the matrix automorphism
1036	  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}\) of a localised Cramer inverse, conjugated by the
1037	  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} on its source and target.
1038	  Transporting to \(V_{IJK}\) along the relevant projection or triple transition by
1039	  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} reassociates the iterated pullbacks
1040	  through the pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}; the resulting
1041	  source, middle, and target sheaves are pinned to a single free sheaf
1042	  \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\) by the three endpoint bridges
1043	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}: the \emph{src} bridge identifies the common source of
1044	  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\), the \emph{mid} bridge the
1045	  target of \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) with the source of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\),
1046	  and the \emph{tgt} bridge the target of \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}\) with that of
1047	  \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). Under these identifications the free-pullback comparisons
1048	  cancel and each transported transition becomes
1049	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse:
1050	  \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^I_J)^{-1})\),
1051	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^J_K)^{-1})\),
1052	  \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J} \mapsto \mathrm{matrixEnd}((X^I_K)^{-1})\). By the linkage
1053	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} the composite of the first two is
1054	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\bigl((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\), and the matrix identity
1055	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} rewrites the argument as \((X^I_K)^{-1}\), so the
1056	  composite equals \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\). The bridges guarantee both sides are
1057	  isomorphisms between the same pair of sheaves on \(V_{IJK}\), so the equality
1058	  is well-formed.
1059	\end{proof}
1060	
1061	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1062	[Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)]
1063	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}
1064	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle}
1065	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1066	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
1067	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1068	  Over each triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\) the base-change transports
1069	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three bundle transitions satisfy
1070	  \[
1071	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
1072	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J},
1073	  \]
1074	  the module-level form of the descent condition (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for
1075	  the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\). This is the \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued multiplicative cocycle
1076	  of \(\mathcal{U}\).
1077	\end{lemma}
1078	\begin{proof}
1079	  \uses{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1080	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1081	  The statement decomposes into three independent steps. First, the matrix-algebra core
1082	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} establishes the Cramer-inverse cocycle
1083	  \((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\) over the triple overlap. Second, the linkage
1084	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} records that composition of matrix automorphisms realises
1085	  matrix multiplication. Third, the transport step \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1086	  carries the three transitions to the common overlap \(V_{IJK}\), aligns their source,
1087	  middle, and target sheaves through the base-change transport and the endpoint bridges, and
1088	  -- feeding it the matrix identity through the linkage -- yields exactly the stated equality
1089	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} = \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\).
1090	\end{proof}

1156	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
1157	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}).
1158	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
1159	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
1160	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
1161	  \[
1162	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1163	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
1164	  \]
1165	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
1166	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and
1167	  surjectivity is local on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally
1168	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
1169	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
1170	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.
1171	\end{definition}
1172	
1173	\section{The functor of points}
1174	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1175	
1176	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1177	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1178	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1179	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1180	identities that make that action functorial.
1181	
1182	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1183	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1184	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1185	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1186	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1187	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1188	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1189	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1190	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1191	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1192	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1193	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1194	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1195	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1196	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1197	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1198	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1199	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1200	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1201	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1202	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1203	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1204	  functor's value at \(T\).
1205	
1206	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1207	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1208	  \[
1209	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1210	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1211	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1212	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1213	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1214	  \]
1215	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1216	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1217	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1218	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1219	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1220	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1221	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1222	  map of equivalence classes.
1223	\end{definition}
1224

1226	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
1227	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.

1239	\end{lemma}

446	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
447	
448	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
449	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
450	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
451	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
452	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
453	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
454	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
455	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
456	morphisms. -/
457	
458	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
459	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
460	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
461	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
462	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
463	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
464	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
465	  constructor
466	  intro V
467	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
468	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
469	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
470	  apply zigzag_isConnected
471	  intro s t
472	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
473	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
474	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
475	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
476	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))

478	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
479	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
480	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
481	Project-local. -/
482	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
483	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
484	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
485	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
486	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
487	
488	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
489	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
490	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
491	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
492	    eqToHom (congrArg
493	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
494	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
495	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
496	  subst h; simp
497	
498	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
499	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
500	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
501	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
502	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
503	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
504	    (I : Type u) :
505	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
506	      = eqToIso (congrArg
507	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
508	          h) := by
509	  subst h; simp

511	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
512	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
513	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
514	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
515	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
516	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
517	Project-local. -/
518	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
519	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
520	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
521	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
522	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
523	  intro i
524	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
525	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
526	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
527	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
528	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
529	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
530

535	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
536	
537	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
538	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
539	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
540	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
541	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
542	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
543	
544	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
545	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
546	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
547	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
548	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
549	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
550	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
551	
552	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
553	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
554	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
555	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
556	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
557	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
558	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
559	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
560	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
561	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
562	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
563	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
564	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
565	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
566	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
567	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
568	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
569	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
570	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
571	
572	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
573	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
574	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
575	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
576	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
577	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
578	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
579	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
580	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
581	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
582	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
583	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
584	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
585	    matrixToFreeIso
586	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
587	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
588	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
589	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
590	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
591	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
592	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
593	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
594	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
595	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
596	
597	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
598	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
599	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
600	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
601	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
602	
603	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
604	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
605	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
606	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
607	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
608	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
609	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
610	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
611	`chartTransition`. -/
612	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
613	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
614	      = eqToIso (congrArg
615	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
616	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
617	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
618	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
619	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
620	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
621	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
622	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
623	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
624	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
625	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
626	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
627	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
628	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
629	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
630	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
631	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
632	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
633	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
634	      = Iso.refl _ := by
635	    apply Iso.ext
636	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
637	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
638	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
639	  simp only [bundleTransition]
640	  erw [hB, Iso.refl_trans]
641	  exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso hφ (Fin d)

643	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the

645	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :

112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1

185	/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
186	`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
187	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
188	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
189	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
190	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
191	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
192	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
193	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
194	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
195	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
196	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
197	    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]

479	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
480	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)

601	/-- **Cocycle condition** (`lem:gr_cocycle`): over the triple overlap, the transition maps satisfy

621	    rw [cocycleΘJK]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _

626	      (((awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp

635	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level charts and the glue data
636	
637	We assemble the affine charts (`affineChart`), their principal-open overlaps
638	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`, the transition isomorphisms (`transitionMap`), and

130	
131	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
132	
133	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
134	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
135	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
136	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
137	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
138	
139	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
140	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
141	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
142	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
143	
144	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
145	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
146	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
147	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
148	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
149	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
150	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
151	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
152	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
153	
154	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
155	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
156	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
157	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
158	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
159	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
160	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
161	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
162	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
163	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
164	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
165	
166	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
167	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
168	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
169	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
170	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
171	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
172	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
173	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
174	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
175	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
176	    congr 1
177	    funext i q
178	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
179	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
180	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
181	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
182	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]
183	
184	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
185	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
186	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
187	  rw [matrixEnd]
188	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
189	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
190	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
191	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
192	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
193	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
194	    by_cases h : p = i
195	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
196	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
197	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]

199	/-- An invertible `d × d` matrix of global sections induces an automorphism of the free
200	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
201	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
202	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
203	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
204	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
205	  hom := matrixEnd M
206	  inv := matrixEnd N
207	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
208	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]
209	
210	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
211	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
212	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl

216	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
217	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
218	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases

220	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
221	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.

223	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
224	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :

227	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)

234	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
235	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom

279	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split

@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :

/-- **Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively** (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`): the
forward maps of two matrix automorphisms compose to `matrixEnd` of the matrix product (with
the order reversed by the column/component contravariance). This is the linkage that turns
the matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module
isomorphisms. Project-local. -/
lemma matrixToFreeIso_mul {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
    (hAA' : A * A' = 1) (hA'A : A' * A = 1) (hBB' : B * B' = 1) (hB'B : B' * B = 1) :
    (matrixToFreeIso A A' hAA' hA'A).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' hBB' hB'B).hom
      = matrixEnd (B * A) := by
  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]

202	theorem universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
204	    (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
205	      = imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ := by
206	  ext p q
207	  simp only [Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix]
208	  by_cases hq : q ∈ J
209	  · rw [dif_pos hq]
210	    set k := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ).symm ⟨q, hq⟩ with hk
211	    have hqk : (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = q := by simp [hk]
212	    have himg : imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ p q = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _) p k := by
213	      have e := congrFun (congrFun (imageMatrix_submatrix_self d r I J hI hJ) p) k
214	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq] at e
215	      rw [← hqk]; exact e
216	    rw [himg, Matrix.one_apply, apply_ite (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ), map_one, map_zero]
217	    have hcond : ((J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) = q) ↔ (p = k) := by
218	      conv_lhs => rw [← hqk]
219	      rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
220	    by_cases hpk : p = k
221	    · rw [if_pos (hcond.mpr hpk), if_pos hpk]
222	    · rw [if_neg (hcond.not.mpr hpk), if_neg hpk]
223	  · rw [dif_neg hq, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X]

/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)

The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.mul_submatrix_col` (private there). -/
private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_map_eq_of_comp` (private there). -/
private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp' {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*}
    [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.isUnit_algebraMap_away_left` (private there). -/
private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
  rw [map_mul] at h
  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel` (private there). -/
private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.imageMatrix_map_eq` (private there). -/
private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
    ← map_nonsing_inv incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
  congr 1
  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` (private there): over the triple
overlap `S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied
entrywise to the image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. -/
private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I K hI hK
    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
    (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
  have hMJimg := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I J hI hJ
    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
    (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
  set Y := (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hY
  have hYJ : IsUnit (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det := by
    have e : (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))
            (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) := by
      rw [hY, Matrix.submatrix_map]
      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
    rw [e]; exact isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' _ _
  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
  have hMJ : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
    have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
          ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
        = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
      rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
      congr 1
      exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
    rw [e1, hMJimg]
  have hRHS : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
    have hmm : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
        = (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
            ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
          * ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
              (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
                (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).map
                  ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
                    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
      rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
    rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, hMJ, universalMinorInv,
      ← map_nonsing_inv _ _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r J K hJ hK), universalMinor,
      map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, hMJ,
      mul_submatrix_col' (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ Y
        (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)),
      Matrix.mul_inv_rev, Matrix.nonsing_inv_nonsing_inv _ hYJ,
      inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' _ _ _ hYJ]
  rw [hLHS, hRHS]

/-- **Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap (L1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix`):
over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` the base-changed Cramer inverses of
the localised minors satisfy the multiplicative cocycle identity
`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹`. This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent
of any sheaf data. Project-local. -/
theorem bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
    (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
      * (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
      = (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
  -- Take the `I`-minor (columns indexed by `I`) of the image-matrix cocycle.
  have hcol := congrArg
    (fun M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away
        (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) =>
      M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)))
    (cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' d r I J K hI hJ hK)
  simp only at hcol
  -- LHS of `hcol` is `(X^I_K)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
  rw [← Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I] at hcol
  -- RHS of `hcol`: reduce `(imageMatrix J K).submatrix I-cols` mapped over to the product.
  rw [← Matrix.submatrix_map] at hcol
  -- `imageMatrix J K = (X^J_K)⁻¹ * X^J`, so its `I`-minor splits off the inverse factor.
  have hsplit : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).submatrix id
        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK * universalMinor d r J I hJ hI := by
    rw [imageMatrix, mul_submatrix_col', universalMinor]
  rw [hsplit, Matrix.map_mul] at hcol
  -- The base change of `X^J_I` over `θ_{I,J}` is `(X^I_J)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
  have hXJI : (universalMinor d r J I hJ hI).map
        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
      = (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
    have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
        = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
      rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
      exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
    rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map,
      universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
  rw [hXJI] at hcol
  exact hcol.symm

/-- **Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)**

/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_nonsing_inv` (private there). -/
private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]

# L3 transport for `bundleTransition_cocycle` (C2)

**Target.** `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle` (GrassmannianQuot.lean,
the `_hC2` form for `Scheme.Modules.glue` at `theGlueData d r` / `bundleTransitionData`).

**Status (iter-061).** L1 + L2 are CLOSED axiom-clean in the file:
- L2 `matrixToFreeIso_mul` — composing two `matrixToFreeIso` forward maps = `matrixEnd (B·A)`.
- L1 `bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix` — the Cramer-inverse cocycle
  `(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring
  `S_I = Localization.Away (minorDet I J * minorDet I K)`, stated with the public ring homs
  `cocycleΘIJ`, `awayInclLeft`, `awayInclRight`. Proved by taking the `I`-minor of the
  ported `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq'`.

C2 has `apply Iso.ext` + a precise roadmap comment + `sorry`. The hom-level goal after
`Iso.ext` is the composite of three `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`s, with each
`bundleTransition I J` unfolded to `pullbackFreeIso (f I J) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso (M_IJ) ≪≫
(pullbackFreeIso (t I J ≫ f J I)).symm`.

## The missing ingredient (net-new infrastructure)

### (a) matrixEnd-under-pullback naturality
For `p : T ⟶ S` (`T S : Scheme.{0}`) and `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S,⊤)`:
```
(Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
  = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
    ≫ matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom p.appTop).mapMatrix M)
    ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv
```
Here `p.appTop : Γ(S,⊤) ⟶ Γ(T,⊤)` is the comorphism on global sections
(`Scheme.Hom.appTop`). Proof route: `matrixEnd M = isoCoproduct.symm.hom ≫
biproduct.matrix (scalarEnd ∘ M) ≫ isoCoproduct.hom`; `Scheme.Modules.pullback p` is a left
adjoint, hence additive and biproduct-preserving, so it commutes with `biproduct.matrix` up
to the coproduct comparison; the crux is the single-entry statement
`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a)` conjugated by the unit-pullback comparison
(`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the `pullbackFreeIso` at a one-element index) equals
`scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`. That is the genuine new lemma — `scalarEnd` naturality under
pullback — and lives squarely in "the diamond" (use term-mode, keep `pullbackComp`/
`pullbackId` opaque).

### (b) Base-change bridge to L1's ring homs
The base-change maps `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤)` induced by the projections / triple
transition `t'` must be identified — via `ΓSpecIso` naturality on the affine charts and the
way `theGlueData` / `chartTransition` are assembled from `transitionMap` — with the ring homs
`cocycleΘIJ` / `awayInclLeft` / `awayInclRight` over which L1 is stated. Then L1 applies to
the base-changed matrices produced by (a).

## Assembly once (a)+(b) land
Each `pullbackBaseChangeTransport` of a `bundleTransition`, after the `pullbackComp`
reassociations and the three `glueData_bridge_*`/`pullbackCongr` casts cancel the
`pullbackFreeIso` comparisons (via (a)), becomes `matrixEnd` of a base-changed Cramer inverse
on `O_{V_IJK}^d`. L2 composes the first two transports to `matrixEnd ((X^J_K)⁻¹(X^I_J)⁻¹)`,
L1 rewrites the argument to `(X^I_K)⁻¹` (after (b)), matching the third transport.

## Note on `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` visibility
L1's proof needed `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` and five matrix helpers
(`mul_submatrix_col`, `map_map_eq_of_comp`, `isUnit_algebraMap_away_left`,
`inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel`, `imageMatrix_map_eq`, `map_nonsing_inv`), all of which are
`private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`. They were ported verbatim into GrassmannianQuot.lean
as `private … '`-suffixed copies. If the Cells owner makes these `theorem`/non-private, the
ports can be deleted and L1 simplified.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean

Iter-061 lane: BUILD + PROVE the C2 chain (L1 → L2 → L3 → C2).

## matrixToFreeIso_mul (L2) — NEW, line ~219
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** blueprint one-liner — `rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED, axiom-clean.
- **Signature:** `(matrixToFreeIso A A' …).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' …).hom = matrixEnd (B * A)`.
  Ready for blueprint `\leanok` on `lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`.

## bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix (L1) — NEW, line ~816
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** state the Cramer-inverse cocycle `(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the
  triple-overlap ring `S_I = Localization.Away (minorDet I J * minorDet I K)`, using the public
  ring homs `cocycleΘIJ`, `awayInclLeft`, `awayInclRight`. Prove by taking the `I`-minor
  (columns indexed by `I`) of `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` and simplifying.
- **Obstacle:** `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` and its matrix helpers (`mul_submatrix_col`,
  `map_map_eq_of_comp`, `isUnit_algebraMap_away_left`, `inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel`,
  `imageMatrix_map_eq`, `map_nonsing_inv`) are ALL `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean` —
  not importable.
- **Resolution:** ported the six helpers + `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` verbatim as `private … '`
  copies into GrassmannianQuot.lean (known-good code; the originals compile in Cells), then
  derived L1. The `I`-minor extraction:
  - LHS of `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq'` → `(X^I_K)⁻¹` via `Matrix.submatrix_map` +
    `imageMatrix_submatrix_I`.
  - RHS → `(X^J_K)⁻¹ * X^J|_I` (split via `imageMatrix` def + `mul_submatrix_col'`); the second
    factor base-changes over `θ_{I,J}` to `(X^I_J)⁻¹` via the comp identity `hχ` +
    `universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` + `imageMatrix_submatrix_I`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED, axiom-clean (`propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`).
  Ready for blueprint `\leanok` on `lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix`.
- **Gotchas logged:**
  - `Matrix.submatrix_map : (A.map f).submatrix e₁ e₂ = (A.submatrix e₁ e₂).map f` (forward
    turns `(map).submatrix` → `(submatrix).map`; I initially used `←` wrongly).
  - `mul_submatrix_col'` will NOT `rw` (HMul pattern miss on the non-square product); apply it
    as a term: `rw [imageMatrix]; exact mul_submatrix_col' _ _ _` (mirrors Cells' own usage).
  - `imageMatrix J K`'s second factor lives over `Away(minorDet J K)`, NOT `universalMinor J I`
    (different ring) — the `I`-minor must stay as `((universalMatrix J).map algebraMap).submatrix`.
  - `map_nonsing_inv` is itself private in Cells → ported as `map_nonsing_inv'`.

## bundleTransition_cocycle (C2) — line 891, STILL OPEN (sorry)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** `apply Iso.ext` to drop to the morphism level (compiles; this is the right
  starting shape — `matrixEnd` lives at hom level), then the three `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`s.
- **Result:** PARTIAL. `apply Iso.ext` lands; the body is a documented `sorry` with the full L3
  roadmap inline. L1 + L2 are in hand; the MISSING ingredient is net-new infrastructure:
  - **(a)** matrixEnd-under-pullback naturality:
    `(pullback p).map (matrixEnd M) = pullbackFreeIso.hom ≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop.mapMatrix M) ≫
    pullbackFreeIso.inv` (crux = `scalarEnd` naturality under pullback, in the diamond).
  - **(b)** base-change bridge identifying the scheme-pullback maps `Γ(U^I_J) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK)` with
    L1's ring homs `cocycleΘ`/`awayIncl` (via `ΓSpecIso` naturality + how `theGlueData` is built).
  - Full plan in `informal/bundleTransition_cocycle_L3_transport.md`.
- **Why not closed:** (a)+(b) are ~150 LOC of diamond infrastructure (the planner's anticipated
  "L3 stalls → isolate standalone iter-062"). Not attempted as compiling code this session to
  avoid leaving the file broken; `apply Iso.ext` is the genuine partial step.

## Hygiene
- Did NOT mark `scalarEnd_val_app` etc. `private` this iter (focused budget on the C2 chain;
  they are unmatched helpers, low priority — defer).

## Summary
- **Sorry count:** 4 → 4 (UNCHANGED), but **L1 and L2 are NEW closed theorems** (they did not
  exist before — this was a BUILD + PROVE task). No new sorries introduced; the ported private
  machinery is all proof-complete.
- **Closed (new, axiom-clean):** `matrixToFreeIso_mul` (L2), `bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`
  (L1), plus 7 ported `private` helpers (`mul_submatrix_col'`, `map_nonsing_inv'`,
  `map_map_eq_of_comp'`, `isUnit_algebraMap_away_left'`, `inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel'`,
  `imageMatrix_map_eq'`, `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq'`).
- **Still open:** `bundleTransition_cocycle` (C2, line 891 — L3 transport, see (a)/(b) above),
  `universalQuotient` (rides on C2), `tautologicalQuotient` (rides on universalQuotient), and
  the line-1468 sorry (functor downstream, out of this lane's scope).
- **Adjacent sorries:** universalQuotient/tautologicalQuotient are downstream of C2 and cannot
  be attempted until C2 closes; not in scope this iter.
- **Build:** `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianQuot` succeeds in 24s
  (baseline 22s — no OOM/heartbeat regression from the ~190 added lines).

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** closed L1 (`bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`) and L2 (`matrixToFreeIso_mul`),
  both axiom-clean and kernel-verified, plus 7 supporting helpers. These are the planner's
  explicit fallback deliverable ("leave L1+L2 closed + the best L3 partial"). Sorry count
  unchanged at 4 because L1/L2 were brand-new declarations (BUILD task), not previously-open
  sorries.
- **C2/L3 — Infrastructure missing (named precisely):** C2 needs two net-new pieces of diamond
  infrastructure: (a) the matrixEnd-under-pullback naturality lemma (crux: `scalarEnd`
  naturality under `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, conjugated by `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`), and (b)
  the base-change bridge `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤) ≅ cocycleΘ/awayIncl`. Each is ~50-100 LOC of
  term-mode work under the `X.Modules` diamond. This matches the planner's pre-stated "L3
  stalls → isolate standalone iter-062". I left `apply Iso.ext` + the full roadmap in the proof
  body and `informal/bundleTransition_cocycle_L3_transport.md`. No LLM API key in env, so the
  informal agent was unavailable (documented in PROGRESS standing notes).
- **Cross-file note for the planner:** the seven helpers ported into this file are `private` in
  `GrassmannianCells.lean`. If the Cells owner exports `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` (+ the five
  matrix helpers + `map_nonsing_inv`) as non-private, the ports here can be deleted and L1

Both verified: axioms `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` only.

The blocker is named exactly: two pieces of net-new "diamond" infrastructure — **(a)** matrixEnd-under-pullback naturality (crux = `scalarEnd` naturality under `Scheme.Modules.pullback`), and **(b)** the base-change bridge `Γ(U^I_J)⟶Γ(V_IJK) ≅ cocycleΘ/awayIncl`.
